steps.title: Simplify and identify the condition for no solution
steps.content:
Start with the given equation: $13(x - n) = 13y + 13n$
Expand both sides:
$$13x - 13n = 13y + 13n$$
Combine like terms:
$$13x - 13y = 26n$$
Divide by 13:
$$x - y = 2n$$
Rewrite in slope-intercept form:
$$y = x - 2n$$
This equation has slope 1 and y-intercept $-2n$.

For the system to have no solution, the two equations must represent parallel lines — same slope but different y-intercepts. So the second equation must have slope 1 and y-intercept ≠ $-2n$.

steps.title: Identify the parallel equation among the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: $2x - 2y = 4n$ simplifies to $x - y = 2n$, or $y = x - 2n$. This is identical to the first equation, so the system has infinitely many solutions. ✗
  • Choice B: $2x + 2y = 2n$ simplifies to $x + y = n$, or $y = -x + n$. The slope is -1, not 1, so these lines are not parallel. ✗
  • Choice C: $2x + 2y = 4n$ simplifies to $x + y = 2n$, or $y = -x + 2n$. The slope is -1, not 1, so these lines are not parallel. ✗
  • Choice D: $2x - 2y = 2n$ simplifies to $x - y = n$, or $y = x - n$. The slope is 1 (matching the first equation) and the y-intercept is $-n$, which is different from $-2n$. These are parallel lines with no intersection. That matches choice D — D is correct.
